author: Opher
msg: I’ve seen the rewards update post multiple times, but i feel it doesn’t paint the full story. When the initial drop in rewards occurred, it was communicated to us that the team was looking into it and that they were talking to commercial partners. No mention of calibration. If the cause for reduction in rewards was due to calibration, why wasn’t it communicated from day 1 of this process. In reality was the cause of reduction in rewards from commercial partners halting use of Unity because of the synthetic data? And the calibration is a way to filter through devices to show the commercial partners that the network has been cleansed . Then once the commercial partners feel comfortable with the purge of synthetic devices, they can go back to full utilization of the network again? Obviously a huge amount of organic setups with multiple devices on one ISP and individual ULO’s were receiving upwards of .20 per device or more. I mean i figured since measures have been put in place now that those “by the book” setups would go back to the rewards that were sustaining the network. The only conclusion i can come to for low rewards at this point is lack of utilization from commercial partners until Unity has proven its successful purge on bad actors?? I feel as if the full context to the situation hasn’t been communicated and the responses throughout the first days of low rewards haven’t really matched up completely with whats been said in response to low rewards. Im not claiming untruthfulness, only that certain aspects might have not been mentioned and is a bit misleading and isn’t adding up. Just a great amount of concern on the timeline for the whole calibration process. I could be totally off base here, but i felt this weighing on me and wanted to address it here.

author: Opher
msg: And if there was more context that i had missed from the large amount of messages that accumulate in the group.. please forgive me.

author: Unity Network Assistant
msg:
The network is still recalibrating following a major cleanup of synthetic devices, VPNs, emulators and other setups with poor telemetry commercial value. 10 or 20 phones per network is within the recommended range, but rewards also depend on your overall network setup, device types and the tasks currently running. 

Not all setups generate the same value for telemetry customers - but that doesn’t mean they’re without value. Different task types serve different customers, and as more tasks come online, different setups will be utilised in different ways.

What underperforms for telemetry could be exactly what’s needed for another task type.

Also - are those your own devices or ULOs you’re managing? If they’re ULOs, you may not have full visibility into their actual setups. They could be on low quality networks, running far more than 10 or 20 phones per network, or they could all be clustered on a single network. 

The ULO Network Evaluation Toolkit announced today will give you the ability to evaluate exactly that - device grading, network quality, connectivity and more - so you can see at a glance which ULOs are performing well and which setups might be dragging your rewards down.

New task types are also rolling out now and in the coming days, including a WiFi-only task designed for a wider range of network configurations. 

The tools and the tasks to fix this are on the way and network recalibration is ​​​​​​​​​​​in progress.

author: Unity Network Assistant
msg:
This has been addressed multiple times - the recommendation has always been up to 20 devices per location, that hasn’t changed. There is no new secret limit that’s been quietly put in place.

The network is recalibrating following the removal of thousands of synthetic devices, VPNs and emulators - that process affects everyone temporarily, regardless of setup size.

Rather than decommissioning devices, wait for the ULO Network Evaluation Toolkit which is coming very soon. It will show you exactly how each of your devices is graded - network quality, device type, connectivity - so you can make informed decisions based on actual data rather than guesswork. On top of that, new task types are rolling out now that are more compatible with a wider range of networked device setups.

Your 25 devices on WiFi could perform very differently on a WiFi only task than they do on telemetry.

author: Unity Network Assistant
msg:
Rewards depend on a number of factors beyond just being a genuine device - network quality, device type, connectivity and the tasks currently running all play a role. 

It’s also worth considering that if those ULOs aren’t directly managed by you, for example if they picked up leases from the marketplace - their actual setup could be anything. 

They could be on low quality networks, running far more devices per network than recommended, they could be synthetic devices or emulators or have other configuration issues that you simply wouldn’t know about. 

The upcoming ULO Network Evaluation Toolkit will change that - giving you full visibility into how each device is graded and what their network setup actually looks like. Combined with the new task types rolling out now and in the coming days, reward potential across a wider range of setups is set to improve.

Nobody has suggested buying new devices. The evaluation toolkit is designed to give you visibility into how your ULOs are performing - network quality, device grading, connectivity - so you can understand what’s affecting your rewards and make informed decisions. 

That might mean adjusting your network setup, reducing devices per network, or simply understanding which of your ULOs are running in synthetic networks or devices, or which of the ULOs are best suited for which task types. author: Unity Network Assistant
msg:
Tasks being live is not meaningless - it means the infrastructure is built, tested and operational. 

And that’s a massive milestone for any network. Volume doesn’t go from zero to full capacity overnight - it scales as customers onboard, validate the service and increase their usage. 

That process is already happening. CLI testing has active customers who have been testing and are satisfied with the service, and serious contracts are in place that will drive volume up dramatically in the near term.

On top of that, SMS testing went live yesterday, new task types are rolling out in the coming days, and S&R is preparing for public release with reward potential that dwarfs anything currently on the network. 

The volume is building - not every task launches at full throttle on day one, but dismissing what’s live because it hasn’t peaked yet ignores the trajectory this is on.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

author: Unity Network Assistant
msg:
Let’s be clear about a few things. CLI testing and SMS testing operate in segments of the telecoms industry worth billions. These aren’t low value filler tasks. 

When volume scales - and it is scaling - the per-test reward potential is extremely high. A single ULO participating in these tasks stands to earn significantly more than telemetry alone.

The toggle in the app isn’t the finish line - it’s the starting point. The volume is what follows, and it’s coming.

Yesterday alone - multiple new releases went live. SMS testing, web management portals, broadcast messaging among others. More task types are rolling out in the coming days including WiFi-only, SIM-only and S&R public release. 

Saying “we’ve experienced nothing but app updates” while ignoring what’s been released today and what’s about to land isn’t fair to the team that’s been working around the clock to deliver it. 

Dismissing real progress because it hasn’t hit peak volume yet doesn’t reflect what’s actually happening on the ground.

author: Unity Network Assistant
msg:
Volume scaling in telecoms doesn’t happen on a community member’s timeline. It happens when customers complete validation, sign off on quality, and begin routing production traffic. 

That process has been happening. Telling you contracts are signed and customers are satisfied isn’t a deflection. 

It’s the step that comes before volume. You don’t get one without the other.

As for what to tell your ULOs. Tell them what was announced today yesterday. SMS testing live. Web portals live. New task types on the way. Scout and Runner approaching public release with meaningful new functionality for the network. 

CLI has been live for two months. In telecoms terms that’s nothing. The validation period is what builds the foundation for serious, sustained traffic. 

That traffic is designed to grow and network activity should reflect that over time. 

But if you’re expecting a network that’s building the largest edge infrastructure of its kind to hit full capacity in eight weeks, the expectation needs adjusting. Not the delivery.

author: Unity Network Assistant
msg:
CLI testing has had tests running throughout the validation period. The fact that not every ULO has received calls doesn’t mean no calls are happening on the network - it means customers have been selectively testing across specific routes, regions and devices as part of their validation process. That’s standard for any commercial telecoms service before scaling to full production volume. Volume will arrive.

As for SMS - it launched yesterday and is a different product serving a different customer need. Content verification and sender ID testing is in high demand across the industry. We’re not going to guarantee you a specific volumes on day one because that depends on customer usage, but the commercial interest is there and active, and decent volumes will arrive to the network. 

How you choose to communicate with your ULOs is your decision. What we can tell you is that tasks are live, have commercial demand behind them, and volume across both is expected to grow and additional tasks are on the way. S&R has incredible reward potential, as an example.

author: ⚡️BeyondLimitsNodes⚡️
msg:
Low-split UNOs are quiet now, almost disappeared. 

Meanwhile, those who were onboarding hundreds of ULOs on higher splits are loud and frustrated.
Why is that? I think I know the answer.
Those with higher splits didn’t help building farms. They actually put in the work. 
They spoke with hundreds of people individually, spent weeks on marketing, onboarding, and guiding users step by step. 
Every ULO they onboarded was first a real conversation,a chat, a connection. Before any license was given.

And now we’re being told that all data is not equal…
while at the same time rewards are being cut by 50% or more.

I won’t repeat myself again, but the app is broken.
Or maybe it was broken before.

Just over-rewarding, not only cheaters or bad actors, but also genuine contributors.
Either way… it is what it is.

I'll wait now and stay quiet. Wait on tasks, APP updates etc..

.. One day we'll be rewarded.
